dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Daewon High. A name that once signified academic excellence, now whispered with a different kind of reputation. A place where the curriculum included daily lessons in fear, and the teachers often looked the other way.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It was a school in crisis. And I was here to fix it.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Another one.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLATTER

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: The broken window on the third floor. The graffiti in the stairwell. The hushed whispers of students avoiding eye contact. It's all so painfully familiar.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: MURMUR

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: They're not even trying to hide it anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: You got it, Jin-woo? My 'lunch money'?

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jin-woo
dialogue.line: I... I told you, I don't have any more. My parents cut off my allowance.

dialogue.speaker: Park Sang-ho
dialogue.line: Oh, really? So you're saying you're just going to starve Min-jun hyung, huh? That's cold, man. Real cold.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUMP (a fist hitting the wall next to Jin-woo's head)

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jin-woo
dialogue.line: Ngh!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: Look, I'm a reasonable guy. But if you can't pay, then maybe you can 'work it off'. There's always the school store, right? Or maybe you can 'borrow' some stuff from the teachers' lounge.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Excuse me.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: FOOTSTEPS (calm, deliberate)

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: Who the hell are you?

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: I'm Na Hwajin. I just transferred here. And I couldn't help but overhear.

dialogue.speaker: Park Sang-ho
dialogue.line: Overhear what, punk? This is none of your business. Go back to your old school.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: It becomes my business when it happens in my line of sight. 'Lunch money', you said? And 'working it off'?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: Yeah, so what? We're just having a friendly chat.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Friendly chats usually don't involve threats of property damage or coercion. And they rarely involve one party looking like they're about to wet themselves.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jin-woo
dialogue.line: (Whispering) Please, just go...

dialogue.speaker: Park Sang-ho
dialogue.line: You think you're tough, new kid? You don't know who you're messing with.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Oh, I think I have a pretty good idea. Kim Min-jun, right? Son of Assemblyman Kim Byung-ho. And Park Sang-ho, your father owns the local construction company.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: FREEZE (bullies)

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: How... how do you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: It's my job to know. Or rather, it was. Now, about this 'lunch money'. If I were to report this, not to the school, but directly to the Ministry of Education's Special Anti-Bullying Task Force, how do you think your fathers would react?

dialogue.speaker: Park Sang-ho
dialogue.line: The... the Task Force?

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Yes. The one that's been quietly investigating schools with high rates of unaddressed violence and corruption. The one that has the power to shut down institutions and ruin reputations.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: And I'm sure they'd be very interested in how the sons of prominent figures are contributing to the problem.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: You're bluffing. You're just a student.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Am I? Or am I someone who knows exactly how to make a formal complaint stick, how to gather evidence, and how to ensure it reaches the right desks? Someone who knows the difference between a 'friendly chat' and criminal extortion.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: I suggest you return Lee Jin-woo's property and find another way to fund your lunches. Or perhaps, just eat the school cafeteria food like everyone else.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: GRIT (Kim Min-jun's teeth)

dialogue.speaker: Kim Min-jun
dialogue.line: This isn't over, new kid.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Oh, I assure you, it's just beginning.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RUSTLE (bullies retreating hastily)

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jin-woo
dialogue.line: Th-thank you...

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Don't thank me. Thank yourself for surviving this long. But next time, don't let it get this far.

dialogue.speaker: Na Hwajin
dialogue.line: Daewon High. It's going to be a long semester. But I've dealt with worse. Much, much worse.

summary: Na Hwajin transfers to Daewon High, a school rampant with bullying. He quickly intervenes in an extortion attempt, using his knowledge of the bullies' backgrounds and the Ministry of Education's anti-bullying task force to intimidate them, signaling his arrival and intent.
